leadership.name: Thomas M. Siebel
leadership.title: Chairman & CEO (returned May 2026)
leadership.background: Founder of C3.ai and Siebel Systems (acquired by Oracle for $5.8B). Stepped aside Sep 2025 due to health; resumed CEO after Ehikian's tenure. Pioneered enterprise CRM software in the 1990s.

leadership.name: Ed Abbo
leadership.title: President & Chief Technology Officer
leadership.background: Long-tenured CTO; key architect of C3 Agentic AI Platform.

businessOverview: C3.ai is an enterprise AI software company providing the C3 Agentic AI Platform, industry-specific SaaS AI applications, and generative AI offerings to large enterprises and government agencies.

Founded 2009 by Tom Siebel; IPO December 2020 (NYSE: AI). FY2025 revenue of $389M (+25% YoY) was the high-water mark — FY2026 revenue collapsed to ~$250M (-36%) following a disruptive CEO transition from Siebel to Stephen Ehikian (Sep 2025) and back to Siebel (May 2026).

Federal segment is the standout growth engine: 51 federal agreements closed in FY2025, federal bookings up 100%+ YoY, representing ~20% of total bookings. Federal/defense/aerospace was 45% of Q2 FY2026 bookings.
Primary go-to-market: hyperscaler partnerships (Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google Cloud) plus SI alliances (PwC, McKinsey QuantumBlack) now account for 73% of FY2025 agreements.

Strategic pivot underway to agentic AI: C3 Agentic Process Automation, C3 Code, and C3 Agentic Websites launched in late 2025. Company exploring potential sale or merger (Automation Anywhere talks reported Jan 2026) amid post-transition uncertainty.
brief.productModel: Subscription licenses (~86% of revenue in FY2025) for the C3 Agentic AI Platform and industry-specific applications across manufacturing, energy, healthcare, financial services, and federal. Professional services and prioritized engineering (IPDs) supplement subscriptions but compress gross margins (non-GAAP GM ~69% FY2025 but dropped to ~52% in Q1 FY2026 due to high IPD mix). C3.ai does not report ARR or NRR — moved to consumption/agreement-count metrics.

recentNews.headline: Tom Siebel Returns as CEO; Preliminary Q4 FY2026 Revenue of $51.6M — Down 53% YoY
recentNews.whyItMatters: Siebel's return signals a board vote of no-confidence in the Ehikian transition. Q4 revenue of $51.6M vs. $108.7M a year ago confirms the severity of the go-to-market collapse and makes near-term recovery difficult to underwrite.

recentNews.headline: C3.ai Cuts 26% of Workforce (312 Jobs); Q3 FY2026 Revenue Misses at $53M vs. $76M Estimate
recentNews.whyItMatters: The cost reset under Ehikian was abrupt — acknowledging 'burning too much money' on day one. The 17% same-day stock drop signals a company in operational crisis. Annualized savings of ~$135M buys runway but not credibility.

recentNews.headline: C3.ai Reportedly in Merger Talks with Automation Anywhere
recentNews.whyItMatters: A reverse merger at ~$1.7B market cap signals limited confidence in the standalone path. The deal structure raises dilution and strategic coherence concerns — but for shareholders, M&A optionality may be the best remaining thesis.

recentNews.headline: C3.ai Explores Potential Sale Following Founder CEO Departure
recentNews.whyItMatters: Strategic review launched immediately after Siebel stepped aside — including possible sale or private capital raise. Market's 6% rally on the Reuters report shows investors see more exit value than organic value.

recentNews.headline: Stephen Ehikian Named CEO; C3.ai Withdraws Full-Year FY2026 Guidance on Day One
recentNews.whyItMatters: Pulling guidance on a CEO's first day signals the incoming team had immediate visibility into a deteriorating pipeline — an unusually aggressive reset. Revenue dropped from $108M (Q4 FY2025) to sub-$55M within two quarters.

recentNews.headline: C3.ai Posts Record Q4 FY2025 Revenue of $108.7M (+26% YoY)
recentNews.whyItMatters: This was C3.ai's peak quarter — federal bookings 100%+ YoY, 264 agreements in FY2025. The stark contrast with FY2026's ~$250M underscores how severe the transition disruption was and makes the recovery trajectory highly speculative.

earningsTakeaways.quarter: Q4 FY2025 (most recent completed year) + FY2026 preliminary

keyMetrics.grossMargin: ~69% non-GAAP (FY2025 Q4); ~52% non-GAAP (Q1 FY2026 — IPD mix)
keyMetrics.customers: 264 agreements in FY2025 (+38% YoY); 46 agreements/quarter post-transition

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: Siebel FY2025: 'Breakthrough innovations in agentic AI, dramatically expanded strategic alliances.' Post-transition, Ehikian focused on go-to-market restructuring and a ~$135M cost reduction. Federal/defense is the standout: 45% of Q2 FY2026 bookings. $675M cash provides runway.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Federal demand remains strong (89% YoY growth in gov/defense/aerospace bookings Q2 FY2026). Commercial recovery is uncertain post-sales-reorg. Microsoft partnership pipeline grew 140% YoY in Q1 FY2026 — channel demand building, direct demand rebuilding.

keyRisks: Leadership instability: Siebel → Ehikian → Siebel in under a year destroyed go-to-market continuity and resulted in a 36% revenue decline in FY2026 — recovery trajectory is highly uncertain
Revenue collapse not yet reversed: FY2026 full-year at $250M is below FY2023 levels ($267M) — the company is currently regressing on the top line
M&A uncertainty: Automation Anywhere merger talks and strategic review process create headline risk and management distraction at a critical rebuild moment
Gross margin compression: Q1 FY2026 non-GAAP GM of 52% vs. 69% in Q4 FY2025 — IPD-heavy go-to-market strategy structurally pressures margins
Palantir gap widening: PLTR is profitable, growing 63% YoY, trading at 55x revenue — C3.ai at 3-4x revenue with negative growth is difficult to position as a peer
China/federal concentration: Federal is now 45% of bookings — export control policy shifts and government budget cycles create binary revenue risk

comps.name: Palantir
comps.note: Most direct enterprise AI comp; profitable, growing fast, 55x revenue — the benchmark C3.ai aspires to. Gap has widened significantly.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

slideBullets: C3.ai's FY2026 revenue collapsed to ~$250M (-36% YoY) from $389M in FY2025 — the steepest single-year decline among public enterprise AI software companies — driven entirely by a disruptive founder-CEO transition and sales reorg

Tom Siebel resumed the CEO role in May 2026 after Stephen Ehikian's 8-month tenure; the board is now executing a recovery with $675M cash, a leaner cost structure (-312 headcount), and a refocus on the federal segment (45% of Q2 FY2026 bookings)

Federal/government is the only reliably growing segment — US government/defense/aerospace bookings grew 89% YoY in Q2 FY2026, positioning C3.ai as a de facto enterprise AI infrastructure layer for agency digitization

Agentic AI product pivot (C3 Agentic Process Automation, C3 Code, Agentic Websites) launched in late 2025 targets a higher-multiple, stickier category — but commercial traction has not yet offset the sales disruption

M&A optionality is the near-term investor thesis: Automation Anywhere merger talks (Jan 2026) and a strategic review process suggest the board is weighing all options; at ~$1.2B market cap and $675M cash, the floor valuation is well-supported
diligenceQuestions: What does the commercial pipeline look like under Siebel's return — specifically, are the customer relationships disrupted during the Ehikian tenure (73% of FY2025 agreements came via channel partners) being actively recovered, and what is the current channel partner sentiment?

How sustainable is the federal revenue growth at 45% of bookings? What is the customer concentration within the federal segment (e.g., reliance on a single agency or CDAO vehicle), and what is the ceiling for this segment given C3.ai's current GSA schedule and contract vehicles?

What are the actual terms and current status of the Automation Anywhere merger discussions — is this a live deal, a dead conversation, or a negotiating signal — and how does it impact management bandwidth during the most critical rebuild quarter?
